title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — June 22, 2026
headline: Zero score changes across 20 confirmations as the floor cluster hits its most severe concurrent evidence accumulation of the June series — South Sudan's 2018 peace deal collapse toward total war surfaces for the first time, Sudan's El Obeid drone strikes disable hospitals with 500,000 civilians at named imminent risk, and an OpenAI 42-state AG subpoena five days after its IPO filing opens the largest multistate enforcement action against a frontier AI lab in the benchmark record.
summary: June 22 is a zero-applied, confirmation-dominant cycle: 20 assessments across 3 indexes, 0 score changes, 20 confirmations, and 9 floor or near-floor reinforcements. South Sudan is the highest-magnitude new finding this cycle — President Kiir's abandonment of the 2018 peace deal, Machar's detention on treason charges, SSPDF aerial bombardment of Jonglei health facilities, and a UN commission warning of total-war trajectory are all first-surfaced in this assessment. Sudan carries within-window drone strikes (Jun 22) disabling hospitals in El Obeid as the UNSC demand from Jun 20 remains unanswered. DRC Ebola has reached 896 cases and 232 deaths with 70+ medics infected and confirmed cross-border spread into Uganda; the July natural peak is approximately 5 weeks away. OpenAI (confirmed 27.2) is flagged under pre-adjudication discipline for the 42-state AG subpoena issued five days after its IPO S-1 filing. A Bolivia upgrade proposal was generated and rejected at screening — the 6.3 accurately reflects the elected Paz government's own Jun 8 impunity law, not a stale prior-regime baseline.
